board.title: Board
board.subtitle: Git-based work items flowing inbox → plan → test → done.

dreams.title: Dreams
dreams.subtitle: Founder-customer wishes — features, bugs, ideas & requests.
dreams.wishlistHint: Captured wishes waiting to be triaged.
dreams.empty: Nothing here yet — speak or type a wish below.
dreams.composerPlaceholder: Describe a wish — feature, bug, idea or request…

lockGate.signerSecureEnclaveDesc: This device's built-in biometric (Touch ID / Face ID)
lockGate.biometryHint: One touch to seal this self to this device
lockGate.errUnlockedSessionUnread: Unlocked vault session could not be read — pick yourself again.
lockGate.errIdentityAfterUnlock: Identity unavailable after unlock — try again.
lockGate.errAddDeviceName: Add a device name to continue (e.g. MacBook Air, iPhone).
lockGate.errAddSignerName: Name this signer to continue (e.g. MacBook Air, iPhone).

models.title: Models
models.subtitle: On-device models stored on this machine. The voice model downloads once and then runs fully offline — your data never leaves the device.
models.needsDesktop: On-device models are only available in the desktop app.
models.empty: No models downloaded yet. Open Talk and tap the mic to fetch the voice model.
models.secondaryInstance: Voice transcription runs on the primary instance. This second window shares its model and won't download or load its own copy.

vaultNav.title: Vault
vaultNav.subtitle: Stronghold-backed secrets for this identity.
vaultNav.lockedHint: Unlock your identity in the main window to manage secrets.

vaultSecrets.description: Values are stored in strong.hold on disk. Reveal copies a value into this window only.
vaultSecrets.needsDesktop: Open the AvenOS desktop app to manage Stronghold secrets.

preferences.title: Language
preferences.subtitle: Controls labels and messages in the AvenOS app on this device.
preferences.languageHint: Controls labels and messages in the AvenOS app on this device.
preferences.savedHint: Saved in this vault at self/vault_settings.json on this device only.
preferences.unlockToChange: Unlock your identity to change language settings.

peer.peerNetworkStarting: Peer network is still starting — wait a few seconds after unlock.
peer.trustNewPeerHintHosting: Share the code below — or cancel to start over.
peer.trustNewPeerHintDefault: Show a pairing code they can enter under Join.
peer.generateCodeTitle: Generate a code for another device
peer.noTrustedPeersYet: No trusted peers yet. Tap Trust new peer or enter a code above, then share identities under Self → Share.
peer.removePeerTitle: Remove this peer — stops sync until you pair again
peer.finishingPairing: Finishing pairing and saving your connection on this device.
peer.waitingForOtherDevice: Waiting for the other device — usually up to a minute.
peer.usuallyOneMinute: Usually up to a minute on the open internet.

intents.title: Intents
intents.noIntentSelectedHint: Choose an example intent on the left to preview the main panel.

composer.mobileVoiceHint: Tap to type, double-tap for voice stream, hold to record
composer.micNotAvailable: Microphone not available in this environment

hitl.retrainAria: Re-train intent — open composer with retrain command

display.successMessage: All checks passed — the intent completed successfully.

context.placeholder: Here is the context of the actor — placeholder.

context.intentTransition: Last intent transition at {time} — depends on Skill X, Y, Z

fileDrop.region: Drop files to attach in composer
fileDrop.title: Drop files here
fileDrop.subtitle: Release to open Intents with thumbnails and optional message.
fileDrop.hint: PDF, JPEG, PNG, or SVG — on submit, allowed files sync to your local Groove files table (unlock required).

identities.title: Identities
identities.subtitle: Your identities on this device. Open one to talk or manage todos. To share with someone else, pair under Self → Share.
identities.subtitleLead: Your identities on this device. Open one to talk, manage todos, or choose who can access it under Members.
identities.onlyOneHint: Only see one? Each device starts with its own. Another person's identity shows up after you share access there — keep both apps open for a moment after you allow it.
identities.needsDesktop: Open this screen in the AvenOS desktop app.
identities.unlockToSee: Unlock with Touch ID to see your identities.
identities.noSparksYet: No identities yet — jazz bootstrap normally provisions a default identity after unlock.

talk.title: Talk
talk.subtitle: Messages in {name} — synced over Jazz when peers share this identity.
talk.unlockToSend: Unlock to send and read messages.
talk.noMessagesYet: No messages yet. Say hello — peers with access to this identity will see it after sync.
talk.navNoIdentity: ⚠️ No active identity to act on.
talk.todoNoChange: ⚠️ Nothing to change on that todo.

todos.title: Todos
todos.subtitle: Task list for this identity. Pair under Self → Peers, share access under Self → Share.
todos.unlockToLoad: Unlock with Touch ID to load your database.
todos.notInLedger: No identity matches this id in your ledger —
todos.noTodosYet: No todos in this identity yet.

gallery.title: Files
gallery.subtitle: All files attached to intents or talk messages in this identity. Images and PDFs render a thumbnail; other formats show their file type.
gallery.unlockToLoad: Unlock with Touch ID to load your database.
gallery.notInLedger: No identity matches this id in your ledger —
gallery.noFilesYet: No files for this identity yet. Attach a PDF or image from Intents or Talk.

share.title: Share
share.subtitle: Choose who can access a identity. Pair a device under Self → Peers, then pick a identity and add them.
share.subtitleLead: Choose who can access a identity. Pair a device under
share.subtitleTrail: , then pick a identity and add them.
share.needsDesktop: Sharing needs the AvenOS desktop app.
share.unlockToManage: Unlock to manage identities and sharing.
share.loadingSessionStalled: Still loading session details… If this lasts more than ~{seconds}s, check logs or reload.
share.noSparksBootstrap: No identities yet — bootstrap normally provisions one after unlock.
share.adminsHint: Admins can read and change todos on this identity. Only this device can add or share access with others.
share.didPlaceholder: Paste a member's DID (did:key:… or did:safe:… for non-human types)
share.humanSignerOnly: A Human SAFE admits signer devices only — paste a did:key:… DID, not a did:safe:…
share.addMemberSafeHuman: Add a Human SAFE as member
share.addMemberSafeAven: Add an Aven SAFE as member
share.noEligibleSafes: No eligible SAFEs on this device — paste a did:safe:… DID below.
share.giveAccessHint: Paste a device's DID to share this identity with it. Sharing is the only step — no separate pairing.
share.accessGrantedNote: Access granted — it may take a moment to show on their device.
share.connectedRelay: Connected relay (this device syncs through it):
share.replicaDidPlaceholder: Or paste another server aven's DID (did:key:…)
share.replicaHint: Grant a server aven a blind replication capability: it stores and forwards this identity's encrypted data (durable backup + relay) but receives no key, so it cannot read it. The server prints its DID in its logs.
share.replicateGrantedNote: Replication granted — the server will store and forward this identity's encrypted data.
share.noPairedPeers: No paired peers yet — invite or accept under Self → Peers, then reload this page if needed.
share.noPairedPeersLead: No paired peers yet — invite or accept under
share.noPairedPeersTrail: , then reload this page if needed.
share.everyoneHasAccess: everyone you paired already has access to this identity. Pair another device if you want to add someone else.
share.sparkNotInLedger: That identity id is not in your ledger.
share.revokeConfirm: Stop sharing this identity with {label}? They will stop receiving new changes, but keep whatever they already received.
share.capabilitiesIntro: Every capability on this identity and who holds it — read straight from the biscuit.
share.grantDescOwns: Full control — read, write, and manage members.
share.grantDescReplicate: Blind sync — the aven relays this identity's encrypted data it cannot read (up to 10 MB) to your other devices. It holds no key, so it is not a readable backup.

capDesc.read: Holds the key — can decrypt and read all of this identity's data.
capDesc.directory: Reads only the member directory (who belongs to the aven) — NOT your data. A sync peer holds no data key, so this is all it can ever see.
capDesc.write: Can add and edit the rows this grant covers.
capDesc.delete: Can delete the rows this grant covers.
capDesc.admit: Can add or remove members (write the roster).
capDesc.rotate_dek: Can rotate the encryption key — how access is revoked; the old key can't read new data.
capDesc.replicate: Blind relay — stores & forwards this identity's encrypted data with NO key, so it can never read it.
capDesc.quota: Storage for this identity on this aven is capped at 10 MB — enforced by the aven-node, not bypassable.
capDesc.rate_limit: Inbound writes are rate-limited by the aven-node so one identity can't flood it.
capDesc.owns: Full control — read, write, delete, and manage members.
capDesc.reads: Read-only member — holds the key to decrypt and read, but cannot write.

identities.avenCeoUnclaimedSub: Unclaimed — claim it to become this network's admin/owner.

composer.placeholder: Talk, navigate, or add a todo…

uiLab.title: UI Lab
uiLab.subtitle: JSON view/style demos — QuickJS logic in Tauri, rendered by @avenos/aven-ui.
uiLab.fixtureTodoDesc: Interactive task list — toggle, add, and delete via QuickJS handleEvent.
uiLab.fixtureInvoiceDesc: Read-only invoice — initState only, formatted from source JSON.
uiLab.needsDesktop: Open UI Lab in the AvenOS desktop app for interactive QuickJS sessions.
uiLab.panelSourceHint: Static seed data (domain JSON). QuickJS initState(source) maps this into the initial state object.
uiLab.panelStateHint: Live view-model returned by QuickJS — initState on mount, handleEvent after each interaction. The view binds to $fields here, not to source.

sandbox.fetchWebcmHint: If assets are missing, run from repo root: bun run fetch:webcm

explorer.title: Local database
explorer.subtitle: Grove-backed rows for this device identity via the Rust shell — read-only explorer.
explorer.needsDesktop: Open this screen in the AvenOS desktop app to inspect local Groove rows.
explorer.unlockToLoad: Unlock with Touch ID to load your local database.
explorer.shellNotReady: Local Groove shell did not report ready — the listing may be incomplete. Retry or reload.
explorer.pickTable: Pick a table from the list to inspect rows.
explorer.noReadableRows: No readable rows for this table.
explorer.gatedHiddenTitle: Rows skipped due to biscuit / identity gates (shown only until the next explorer fetch)

self.title: Local database
self.subtitle: AvenOS keeps durable state under your OS Documents folder in .avenOS/ceo.aven/testnet/abagana/ (e.g. ~/Documents/.avenOS on macOS). Groove data lives in identities/<slug>/db/storage.rocksdb; device identity material lives in identities/<slug>/vault/.
self.needsDesktop: Open the AvenOS desktop app to manage local storage paths.
self.clearDescription: Clearing removes the Groove database for the active identity (todos, identities, peers, etc.). Your hardware identity under vault/ is unchanged. After clearing, unlock again and open Todos or DB so the store is recreated.
self.clearSuccess: Local database cleared. Re-open Todos or trigger bootstrap to create a fresh store.
self.confirmClearQuestion: Delete everything under the database folder?
self.fullResetDescription: Full reset deletes the entire .avenOS folder — all vaults, device identity keychains, trusted peers, identities, and schema cache. You will return to the lock screen and must register or unlock again. This cannot be undone.
self.fullResetSuccess: All AvenOS local data removed. Register or unlock to start fresh.

errors.peerNetworkStartingAccept: Peer network is still starting — wait a few seconds after unlock, then try again.
errors.grooveShellNotReady: Local Groove shell is not ready yet.
errors.useSelfContextMissing: useSelfContext: provider missing — wrap pages in /settings/+layout.svelte

identity.title: Self
identity.subtitle: Your identity on this device — {device}.
identity.signerDidDescription: HKDF-derived Ed25519 identity — used for signing, caps, and sync.
identity.unlockToDeriveDid: Unlock to derive your signer DID.

peers.title: Peers
peers.subtitle: Trust another device on your account, then share identities with them.

network.title: Network
network.subtitle: Which Aven network this app is bound to, and how it connects for P2P.
network.currentNetworkDescription: Every device on this build uses the same network. Your vault data and identity are scoped to it.
network.networkNotSwitchable: This device is bound to this network. Network switching is not available in this build.

peers.title: Peers
peers.subtitle: Devices you sync with. Sharing is per identity — grant access under a identity's Members.
peers.shareHint: Share this ID so someone can add you to a identity (Identities → Members → Give access).
peers.none: No peers yet — add one by its DID above.
peers.noneRoster: No one yet — share a identity under Identities → Members to start syncing with a device.
peers.copyDebugHint: Copy recent logs + peer/mesh state to clipboard for bug reports

editor.title: Editing
editor.subtitle: Video clips rendered by the video-edit skill (Hyperframes). Generate new clips from Claude Code; they appear here.
editor.emptyHint: Run the video-edit skill in Claude Code to render one.

bookkeeping.title: Bookkeeping
bookkeeping.subtitle: DATEV SKR04 standard chart of accounts (gültig 2026). Look up which account a transaction must be booked to — search by account number or name.
bookkeeping.funktionHint: DATEV function code (AM/AV = Automatikkonto, S = Sammelkonto, F = general, KU = no VAT calc)

avens.subtitle: Hardcoded example avens. Open one to explore its sections.
avens.sparksSubtitle: Identities this aven has access to. Open one to work inside it.
avens.noSparksYet: No identities yet for this aven.

dashboard.subtitle: How much cash you pull out per hour worked — and the next move to level it up.

banking.subtitle: Balances and transactions across every payment & bank provider — filter by provider on the right.

orders.subtitle: Recent orders from the point-of-sale journal.
orders.importDuplicate: Already imported — nothing new to add.
orders.importEmpty: No rows found in this file.

orderTable.subtitle: Imported order line items as a flat table.
orderTable.empty: No data yet — import a CSV to populate the table.

turnover.subtitle: Gross turnover (brutto Umsatz) accumulated per month.
turnover.empty: No data yet — import a CSV to see monthly turnover.

products.subtitle: Every position by unit price — net, gross, tax, and total sales.
products.empty: No data yet — import a CSV to see product sales.

ingest.subtitle: Watch the import pipeline run, stage by stage, with logs.
ingest.noLogs: No logs yet — import a CSV to see the pipeline run.

humanGate.title: What should we call you?
humanGate.body: Your signer and vault are ready. Now set up your human self — the identity that holds your data and that the network invites in.
humanGate.createOptionDesc: Start fresh — name yourself and mint a new human identity on this device.
humanGate.pairOptionDesc: This is another device of a self you already have — join it from your other device.
humanGate.pairBody: On a device that already holds your human self, open Members and add the signer below as an owner. Once it lands, this device joins automatically.
humanGate.pairWaiting: Waiting for your other device to add this signer…
humanGate.footnote: You can add more selves (avens, sparks) later. This first one is you.

networkGate.title: The new world is built by sovereign founders.
networkGate.body: This is the backbone of a different world — one owned by the people who build on it. A new breed of founders who hold their own identity, their own data, their own word. It isn't a product you sign up for. It's a circle you're welcomed into.
networkGate.how: Share your human SAFE ID below with someone already here. Access is passed hand to hand, founder to founder — they vouch for your human self and open the door.
networkGate.footnote: When a founder invites you, your place will be waiting — and if you're the very first here, you'll be welcomed in automatically.
